title: abrus pulchellus subsp. suffruticosus
intro: Abrus pulchellus subsp. suffruticosus, also known as the small-leaf crab's eye or Java rose, is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain. The plant is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a decoction, infusion, or powder, which can be used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Abrus pulchellus subsp. suffruticosus as a medicinal plant.
